channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Stanford researchers map ‘White-Only’ properties using AI
item.description: Racially restrictive covenants are clauses in property deeds that ban individuals of a particular race from buying a home.
item.dc:creator: Hamza Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Van Halen's unreleased music
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Donald Trump is losing on TikTok
item.description: TikTok’s influence has become undeniable in the 2024 presidential election, with both camps leaning full force into the idea that if you can’t beat ’em, join ’em. But that plan hasn’t worked out so well for Republican nominee Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How AI can help federal agencies make the most of limited budgets
item.description: In today’s budget environment, agency leaders are often asked to do more with less. The post How AI can help federal agencies make the most of limited budgets first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: TikTok approved election misinformation ads despite its own ban, says watchdog
item.description: Just weeks before the U.S. presidential election, TikTok approved advertisements that contained election disinformation even though it has a ban on political ads, according to a report published Thursday by the nonprofit Global Witness.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Get your nominations in for the Oscars of federal technology transfers
item.description: "Technology's changed quite a bit since the 80s. In 2024, we had 32 award winners we recognized from eight federal agencies," said Jesse Midgett. The post Get your nominations in for the Oscars of federal technology transfers first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: Ultimate guide to the best AI tools to make your life and work easier
item.description: Generative AI tools have been popping up everywhere and becoming second nature to use. That’s thanks in part, to a familiar user experience.
item.dc:creator: Sadiya Kherani, Fast Company

item.title: Augmented reality screens are a feature, not a product
item.description: Apple released a claustrophobic submarine movie for Apple Vision Pro earlier this month, and it gave me a sinking feeling. The movie Submerged was neither augmented reality (AR) nor virtual reality (VR) nor 360-degree video. It was more like 180-degree video.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI stagnation: The gap between AI investment and AI adoption
item.description: AI continues to shape cloud strategies, but AI implementation is going slower than most predicted. This is frustrating for technology providers who have made big bets on AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: An Alabama man has been arrested for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hit the lights: Mets outfit Citi Field for electric light shows
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets are lighting up Citi Field, even before their batters step up to the plate. About 70 front-office staffers scattered around the ballpark for over five hours to install 3,500 strips of LED lights on each railing ahead of New York's first home postseason game.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Instagram rolls out new features to prevent sextortion scams targeting teens
item.description: Instagram has introduced a series of new safety features to prevent sextortion schemes, marking the platform's latest effort to protect users, especially teenagers, from online harm.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Get started with the free-threaded build of Python 3.13
item.description: The single biggest new feature in Python 3.13 is something Python users have anticipated for ages: a version of Python that allows full concurrency between Python threads by removing the Global Interpreter Lock.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Voter beware: Text scam targets you, no matter where you live
item.description: Reports warn of a scam text falsely claiming that recipients aren’t registered to vote. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals what you need to know and five tips to avoid such schemes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hide and lock your apps on your iPhone with iOS 18
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explores the iOS 18’s new features to hide and lock apps for better privacy and organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At the mercy of social media
item.description: I like Instagram as much as the next guy. I check in daily on my carefully curated feed of folks I follow and then work hard not to doomscroll the day away. One of my favorite follows is an adorable Corgi (aren’t they all adorable?) named Parsnip.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The tech consultant charged in Cash App founder Bob Lee's stabbing death had no motive to kill him and in fact was forced to defend himself against Lee, who had become aggressive while on a multiday drug bender, lawyers for Nima Momeni said in opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina's mountains, the constant din of a gas-powered generator is getting to be too much for Bobby Renfro.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Top 5 methods to equip your car with Wi-Fi
item.description: Having Wi-Fi has become a necessity for many of us. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explores the top five ways to equip your vehicles with Wi-Fi.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
